answer:
**Manusmṛti** (particularly Chapter 12) extensively theorizes moral action (*karma*) as the cause of rebirths, social stations, heavenly rewards, and hellish punishments. Good actions from body, speech, and mind yield high births (e.g., gods via *sāttvic* deeds), while evil ones lead to low births (e.g., beasts via *tāmasic* deeds) or hells.[^1] Penances expiate sins to avert bad karma's full fruits.[^2]

**Kuñjarakarṇa Dharmakathana** narrates karma's inexorable rewards/punishments: good deeds yield heaven but exhaust, leaving evil karma for hell (e.g., Pūrṇavijaya's heavenly kingship ends in Yama's cauldron for past sins); rebirths match deeds (leprosy for evil, heaven for charity).[^3] Bodhisattva teachings emphasize karma's fruits across realms.[^4]

**San Hyan Tattvajnana** details karma via *guṇas* (*sattva/rajas/tamas*): good karma (*śubhakarma*) yields divine/human births; evil (*aśubhakarma*) yields hell/*triyak*/deformed humans. Knowledge burns karma for *mokṣa*.[^5]

**San Hyan Kamahāyanikan** frames moral action in Mahāyāna: wholesome deeds (*śubhakarma*) exhaust karma for nirvāṇa; evil yields suffering. *Samaya*-vows yield siddhis/Buddhahood as reward.[^6]

**Āṅgirasa Smṛti** links ritual/moral lapses (e.g., impure *karma*) to fruitless rites/outcaste status, with penances restoring merit.[^7]

**Parāśara Smṛti** prescribes recompense via penances proportional to sins (e.g., outcaste for *dharma*-aversion), implying karmic restoration.[^8]

No other works centrally address this; passing mentions (e.g., Gaṇapatitattva on karma-fruits) are incidental.

[^1]: Manusmṛti, Ch. 12, [translation](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/12/translation/#p-074f6e).
[^2]: Manusmṛti, Ch. 12, [translation](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/12/translation/).
[^3]: Kuñjarakarṇa Dharmakathana, Ch. 1, [¶ p-50854d](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-50854d).
[^4]: Kuñjarakarṇa Dharmakathana, Ch. 1, [¶ p-f6e5e8](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-kunjarakarna-dharmakathana-894f4a/01/translation/#p-f6e5e8).
[^5]: San Hyan Tattvajnana, Ch. 1, [¶ p-b23c9d](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-san-hyan-tattvajnana-1f29bd/01/translation/#p-b23c9d).
[^6]: San Hyan Kamahāyanikan, Ch. 1, [¶ p-1ab091](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-san-hyan-kamahayanikan-2a0c19/01/translation/#p-1ab091).
[^7]: Āṅgirasa Smṛti, Ch. 1, [¶ p-ed467c](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-angirasa-smrti-2bf9d1/01/translation/#p-ed467c).
[^8]: Parāśara Smṛti, Ch. 4, [¶ p-5f9454](https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-parasara-smrti-2259be/04/translation/#p-5f9454).
